daily_brief: The day's global developments center on intensifying diplomatic maneuvering across multiple fronts. In Beijing, Presidents Xi Jinping and Vladimir Putin reaffirmed their 'unyielding' strategic partnership [RFI, France 24, Al Jazeera, ABC Australia], signing over 20 agreements just days after President Trump's visit yielded no formal deals—a contrast closely noted by analysts. The EU moved to implement its trade pact with the United States ahead of Trump's July 4 deadline, with lawmakers agreeing to cut duties on US industrial goods in exchange for maintaining 15 percent tariffs on European exports [RFI, Le Monde]. The WHO declared a public health emergency of international concern as the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo reached 131 deaths and over 500 suspected cases, with cross-border spread reported [RFI, Guardian, BBC]. US Secretary of State Rubio criticized the WHO's response timing while defending America's reduced engagement following its withdrawal from the organization [Guardian]. Meanwhile, the US-Iran conflict entered its 82nd day with Trump claiming a deal is imminent, as Chinese tankers finally exited the Strait of Hormuz [Al Jazeera, Globe and Mail, The Hindu]. G7 finance ministers meeting in Paris pledged cooperation despite visible strains over trade policy and Russia [RFI].

top_stories.headline: Xi and Putin Reaffirm Strategic Partnership in Beijing, Contrast with Trump Visit Evident
bullets: Chinese President Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin to Beijing with full state honors, where the two leaders signed more than 20 agreements covering trade, technology, and energy cooperation
Xi described China-Russia relations as demonstrating 'unyielding' resilience, while Putin praised the 'unprecedented' level of bilateral ties despite 'unfavourable external factors'
The summit follows Trump's visit to Beijing last week, which produced no formal deals, press conference, or joint communique—a contrast highlighted by multiple outlets
Energy security dominated discussions, with analysts expecting progress on the Power of Siberia 2 natural gas pipeline as an alternative to disrupted Middle East supplies

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Putin's weakened position due to sanctions and dependence on China

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on the ceremonial aspects and direct comparison with Trump visit

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Highlighted the 'comprehensive partnership' language and strategic coordination

sources.name: ABC Australia
sources.framing_note: Explicitly contrasted the number of deals signed versus Trump's visit, noting Xi's 'balancing act'

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized China propping up Russia's economy amid Western sanctions

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Noted Xi's call for ceasefire in West Asia during the talks

top_stories.headline: WHO Declares Global Emergency as DRC Ebola Outbreak Reaches 131 Deaths, Crosses Borders
bullets: The World Health Organization declared a public health emergency of international concern as the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo reached 131 confirmed deaths and over 500 suspected cases
The outbreak, driven by the Bundibugyo strain for which no approved vaccine exists, has spread beyond its original hotspot in Ituri province and crossed into Uganda, where one death has been reported

US Secretary of State Marco Rubio criticized the WHO for being 'a little late' to identify the outbreak, while experts noted the WHO is operating with severely reduced capacity following US withdrawal
Health officials acknowledge they are playing catch-up with a virus that may have been circulating undetected before the April 24 discovery; severe shortages of basic protective equipment persist

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on WHO declaration and case numbers

sources.name: The Guardian
sources.framing_note: Highlighted Rubio's criticism while noting US withdrawal gutted WHO capacity; emphasized expert rebuttals

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Featured ground-level reporting from affected areas, focusing on local fear and response challenges

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized weak infrastructure hampering response in war-torn region

top_stories.headline: EU Agrees to Implement US Trade Pact Ahead of Trump's July 4 Deadline
bullets: The European Parliament and Council reached provisional agreement to remove duties on US industrial goods and grant preferential access to American agricultural and seafood products
In exchange, the United States will maintain tariffs of 15 percent on most EU exports under the framework agreed at Trump's Turnberry resort last July
Trump had threatened to raise tariffs on European car imports from 15 to 25 percent if the bloc failed to implement the agreement by July 4

The legislation had been delayed twice—once after Trump threatened tariffs on allies opposing his Greenland acquisition proposal, and again after the US Supreme Court struck down earlier global tariffs

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed the negotiation history and Trump's deadline pressure

top_stories.headline: Trump Claims Iran Deal Imminent as Chinese Tankers Exit Strait of Hormuz
bullets: Two Chinese oil supertankers carrying approximately 4 million barrels of crude navigated out of the Strait of Hormuz after waiting more than two months, shipping data showed
President Trump told lawmakers the 82-day US-Israel war on Iran will end 'very quickly,' claiming he was 'an hour away' from ordering new strikes before pausing
Vice President Vance said negotiations have made 'a lot of progress' but acknowledged difficulties dealing with Iran's fractured leadership
The US Senate advanced a war-powers resolution 50-47 to end the Iran war without Congressional authorization, with four Republicans joining Democrats

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Detailed Iranian military warnings of 'new fronts' and Tehran's peace proposal terms

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Senate rebuke of Trump and Constitutional war powers debate

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Included Xi's call for 'comprehensive ceasefire' during Putin talks

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Brief coverage noting Iran says it's 'ready' if diplomacy fails

top_stories.headline: G7 Finance Ministers Pledge Cooperation Amid Visible US-Allied Strains Over Russia and Trade
bullets: G7 finance ministers and central bank governors met in Paris, pledging renewed multilateral cooperation to address threats to global economic stability from the Middle East war
French Finance Minister Roland Lescure described discussions with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent as 'frank, sometimes difficult' but productive
European nations expressed growing unease over Trump administration tariffs and what some perceive as a softening US stance toward Russia
The final communiqué reaffirmed commitment to impose 'severe costs' on Russia, though the UK simultaneously loosened Russian oil sanctions citing supply concerns

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Emphasized tensions between US and allies while noting productive outcome

sources.name: BBC Europe
sources.framing_note: Reported UK sanctions loosening as separate story, noting contradiction with G7 statement

additional_stories.headline: US increases white South African refugee admissions to 17,500, claiming 'emergency situation'
Trump's Greenland envoy Jeff Landry begins first visit to Arctic territory amid continued tensions over acquisition threats
UK loosens Russian oil sanctions as jet fuel and diesel prices rise due to Strait of Hormuz disruption
Samsung Electronics faces major strike threat as South Korean labor minister intervenes in bonus dispute
Taiwan's President Lai marks second anniversary, says island's future will not be decided by 'external forces'
Estonia says NATO fighter jet shot down drone over its territory, suspects Ukrainian projectile redirected by Russian jamming
San Diego mosque shooting suspects identified as radicalized white supremacists who met online
Indonesia creates new state enterprise to control exports of palm oil, coal, and key minerals
Trump-backed challenger defeats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky GOP primary
Arsenal win Premier League title for first time since 2004 after Manchester City draw
